name: Marinated Eggplant
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the eggplant into slices about 3/4-inch thick.
2. Arrange the slices on a dishtowel and sprinkle with salt. Let rest for about 10 minutes to allow water to leach out. Wipe dry and set aside.
3. In a bowl, beat eggs with parsley, a pinch of salt, and pepper.
4. Heat 2 cups oil in a deep skillet.
5. Lightly flour eggplant slices, then dip in the egg wash. Cook in hot oil for about 1 minute per side until golden brown. Place on absorbent towel to cool.
6. In a heavy saucepan with remaining oil, saute crushed garlic until golden.
7. Remove from heat and add vinegar, bay leaves, rosemary (if using), and pepper. Return to heat and simmer for 1 minute.
8. Assemble eggplant layers in a bowl with lid, sprinkling liberally with marinade between each layer. Pour remaining marinade over the top.
9. Cover and turn upside down to mix marinade evenly. Let stand at room temperature for 2 hours (up to 2 days or 2 weeks refrigerated).
10. Unmold onto a serving plate and serve at room temperature.
